evals.prompt: I just installed OpenCode on WSL but 127.0.0.1:4096 won't load — it just times out. I can run `opencode` from the terminal fine but the web UI doesn't work. Can you get it working?
evals.expected_output: Full setup flow: detect environment, create systemd service, deploy Windows launcher, install plugins, start service, verify. No unnecessary prompts to the user.
expectations: Runs environment detection commands (whoami, binary check, DB check, service check, Windows username)
Creates systemd service unit file at ~/.config/systemd/user/opencode-web.service
Runs daemon-reload, enable, and enable-linger
Deploys OpenCode-WSL.bat launcher to Windows Desktop
Writes plugin config to ~/.opencode/opencode.json with all 10 plugins
Clones superpowers repo if not present
Configures Context7 MCP with command as JSON array (not string)
Starts service and verifies it is active
Presents a clean summary at the end
Does NOT ask the user unnecessary questions — infers from environment

evals.prompt: My OpenCode sessions keep disappearing after I restart WSL. I can see the session folders still exist in ~/.local/share/opencode/sessions/ but the web UI sidebar is completely empty. This is driving me crazy.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the DB path mangling bug, runs the fix script automatically without asking for confirmation, and restarts the service.
expectations: Identifies this as the DB path mangling bug (not a generic 'clear cache' suggestion)
Runs opencode_db_fix.py with --dry-run first to diagnose
Auto-applies the fix with --apply without asking user for permission
Mentions that a backup was created automatically
Restarts the opencode-web service after the fix
Does NOT suggest deleting session data or reinstalling OpenCode

evals.prompt: I want the full plugin stack for OpenCode — swarm, memory, safety net, Context7, superpowers, everything. I'm on WSL. Just set it all up for me.
evals.expected_output: Stops service, installs all 10 plugins plus Context7 MCP, clones superpowers, restarts service. Uses array format for Context7 command.
expectations: Stops the opencode-web service before editing config files
Writes all 10 plugins to ~/.opencode/opencode.json plugin array
Uses absolute path for superpowers plugin (/home/<user>/superpowers)
Clones superpowers from github.com/obra/superpowers if not present
Configures Context7 MCP in ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json
Context7 command is a JSON array ['npx', '-y', '@upstash/context7-mcp'], NOT a string
Preserves existing keys in config files (merge, not overwrite)
Restarts service after config changes
Verifies service is active after restart
